article.title: Workday's Agentic AI Revenue Approaches $500 Million ARR as Agent Adoption Doubles Quarter-Over-Quarter
article.summary: Workday Q1 FY2027 results show agentic AI ARR nearing $500M, new ACV up 200% YoY, with 4,000+ customers using at least one company-built agent.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Workday reported first-quarter fiscal 2027 results on May 21, 2026, that beat analyst expectations on both the top and bottom lines while marking a decisive acceleration in its AI business. Annualized revenue from agentic AI solutions is now approaching $500 million, new annual contract value from those products grew more than 200 percent year-over-year, and the number of customers running at least one company-built agent more than doubled in a single quarter.

Co-founder and CEO Aneel Bhusri — who returned to lead the company earlier in 2026, replacing Carl Eschenbach — told analysts it was "the best first quarter of new ACV growth in five years," adding: "Following slower ACV growth in fiscal year 2026, we're seeing momentum once again building in the business," [per Yahoo Finance's earnings call highlights](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

## Financial Results

Total revenues for the quarter ended April 30, 2026 reached $2.542 billion, up 13.5 percent year-over-year, [according to the official Workday press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html). Subscription revenues — the engine of the SaaS business — grew 14.3 percent to $2.354 billion. Non-GAAP operating income was $809 million, a 31.8 percent margin.

Non-GAAP diluted earnings per share came in at $2.66, beating analyst consensus estimates of approximately $2.51, [according to SiliconAngle](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/21/strong-demand-agentic-ai-offerings-helps-workday-beat-expectations-boost-stock-price/). On a GAAP basis, EPS reached $0.87, as net income rose to $222 million from $68 million in the prior-year quarter.

The 12-month subscription backlog grew 15.5 percent to $8.806 billion, while the total subscription backlog reached $27.294 billion, up 10.9 percent, [the press release stated](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html). Operating cash flow rose 52 percent to $696 million, while free cash flow increased 46 percent to $616 million. Workday repurchased approximately 12 million shares for $1.6 billion during the quarter.

Customer expansions drove roughly 60 percent of subscription revenue growth, [according to Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

## AI Agent Surge

The defining story of Workday's Q1 was the pace of its AI agent business. Gerrit Kazmaier, President of Product and Technology, stated that new ACV from agentic AI products grew more than 200 percent year-over-year and that annualized revenue from those solutions is now approaching $500 million, [per Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

More than 4,000 customers are now using at least one organically developed agent — a figure that more than doubled quarter-over-quarter, [according to the official press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html). Workday operates across a platform of more than 80 million users under contract and approximately 1.4 trillion annual transactions, [per Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

Specific agent performance metrics illustrate the deployment scale. The Recruiting Agent supported 14 million hiring processes during Q1, up 44 percent year-over-year, while Contract Intelligence analyzed 1.1 million contracts, [the press release noted](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html).

Kazmaier framed the strategic shift in stark terms during the earnings call, telling analysts that "the 150th feature in HR or finance is not going to move the needle for our business. The next agentic application will," [according to SiliconAngle](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/21/strong-demand-agentic-ai-offerings-helps-workday-beat-expectations-boost-stock-price/). Expansion deals that included AI were more than 50 percent larger on average than non-AI deals, [per Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

## Sana Platform and New Products

Workday's acquisition of Sana Labs, completed in late 2025, has become the company's primary vehicle for what it describes as ["superintelligence for work"](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/introducing-sana-from-workday-superintelligence-for-work-that-finds-answers-takes-action-and-automates-workflows-302715971.html) — a platform that combines search, reasoning, and workflow automation in a conversational interface layered on top of Workday's core HR and finance systems.

The Sana Self-Service Agent launches with more than 300 skills across HR and finance areas, and Sana Enterprise includes connectors for 18 platforms including Box, Confluence, Gmail, Google Drive, Jira, Microsoft Outlook, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SharePoint, Slack, and Zoom, [per the Sana announcement](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/introducing-sana-from-workday-superintelligence-for-work-that-finds-answers-takes-action-and-automates-workflows-302715971.html). Kazmaier described the goal in the Sana announcement: "Sana from Workday will be the last software you have to learn – a single experience where AI is embedded directly in the flow of work."

New products announced alongside the Q1 results included:

- **Sana for ITSM** — an agent that automates employee onboarding, offboarding, access changes, and IT service requests by drawing on Workday's employee lifecycle data
- **Sana Travel Agent** — which integrates business travel planning, booking, and expense management into a single conversational interface
- **Workday Agent System of Record** — now generally available, providing enterprises with visibility and control over all deployed agents
- **Personnel Action Request Agent** — targeting federal HR modernization
- **Military Skills Mapper** — designed to identify and recruit veteran talent

Workday also announced that Sana is integrated into Microsoft 365 Copilot, expanding reach for enterprises already on Microsoft's productivity stack, [the press release noted](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html).

On the implementation side, the Deployment Agent is targeting an estimated 30 percent reduction in implementation hours in current projects, and as much as 50 percent in future AI-driven projects, [per Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/workday-q1-earnings-call-highlights-220640491.html).

## Geographic and Customer Expansion

Workday now counts more than 11,500 organizations worldwide as customers, including more than 65 percent of the Fortune 500, [according to the press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html). Notable new customers in Q1 included ACHM Hotels by Marriott, Australian Gas Infrastructure Group, Del Monte Fresh Produce, Smiths Group, and the State of Delaware, while Bank OZK, GE Vernova, and Queensland University of Technology expanded their existing relationships.

The company expanded into Vietnam — its sixth ASEAN market — and launched EU-based data residency in Frankfurt during the quarter. Workday was named a KLAS Research Best in KLAS ERP winner for the ninth consecutive year.

## Guidance and Strategic Outlook

For fiscal year 2027, Workday reiterated its subscription revenue outlook of $9.925 billion to $9.950 billion, representing 12 to 13 percent growth, while raising its non-GAAP operating margin target to 30.5 percent, [according to the press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/workday-announces-fiscal-2027-first-quarter-financial-results-302779478.html). For Q2, the company guided to subscription revenues of $2.455 billion, representing 13 percent growth.

Bhusri summarized the company's position in a statement accompanying the results: "Our core business is strong, our AI strategy is working, and we're moving with the speed and focus required to lead."

Shares of Workday rose more than 10 percent in after-hours trading following the results, [according to SiliconAngle](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/21/strong-demand-agentic-ai-offerings-helps-workday-beat-expectations-boost-stock-price/), despite the stock having declined 43 percent year-to-date heading into the report.

## What We Don't Know

Workday has not disclosed what share of the approaching $500 million in agentic AI annualized revenue comes from net-new deals versus upgrades of existing customers to AI-inclusive pricing tiers. The company has also not specified the per-customer economics of its Flex Credits AI monetization model beyond noting that AI-included expansion deals average over 50 percent larger than non-AI deals. It remains unclear whether the Deployment Agent's 30-50 percent efficiency targets will compress or shift professional services revenue over time.
